summary: Tests cover all four main retention policies (max-age, keep-latest, max-total-size, combined) and dry-run mode. Error handling for CLI and malformed TSV is tested. However, rigor is limited: edge cases like invalid date/size formats, empty input, boundary values (N=0, max-total-size=0), policy argument validation, and artifact tiebreaker logic are untested. Design uses external fixture files (not shown/created in test code) which creates fragility, and output validation relies on loose string matching (e.g., `[[ "$output" == *"KEEP"*"build-logs-1"* ]]`) rather than exact format or ordering validation. Tests would catch gross failures (policy not working, script crash) but likely miss subtle bugs (incorrect sort order within workflows, edge case handling, exact output format).
